title: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Austin Peay State University)

communications.subject: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Austin Peay State University)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Greetings,

I am looking to make a request for records related to your athletic department.

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

Best,

Greg Chick

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Greg Chick.

title: EscambiaVotes GIS Mapping Data & IT Administration (2022-Present)

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: EscambiaVotes GIS Mapping Data & IT Administration (2022-Present)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

To the Public Records Custodian / Hon. Robert Bender, Escambia County Supervisor of Elections:

Under the Florida Sunshine Law (Chapter 119, F.S.), I am requesting access to and copies of the following public records regarding the public-facing election results portal hosted on or linked via EscambiaVotes.gov.

Publicly available data on the EscambiaVotes portal indicates a systemic, multi-year degradation of the GIS precinct mapping module. Specifically, the public map functioned correctly during the 2022 General Election (last updated 12/8/2022). However, it suffered a complete failure during the 2024 Primary and General Elections (displaying no map data), and remains only partially functional as of the 2025 Special Election (displaying broken or unlinked shapefiles).

Because the underlying tabular voting data is actively present on the site, this request seeks to audit the administrative failure of the GIS mapping API and the visual transparency tool itself. I am requesting the following records:

1. IT and Administrative Correspondence (Dec 2022 - Present):
Any internal IT helpdesk tickets, error logs, or administrative memorandums generated between December 8, 2022, and the present date regarding GIS shapefile errors, precinct redistricting desynchronization, or the failure of the mapping API to visually populate precinct data on the public portal.

2. Third-Party Vendor Communications:
Any communications (including emails, letters, or service requests) between the Escambia County Supervisor of Elections office and the third-party election software vendor/portal host (e.g., VR Systems) dated between December 8, 2022, and the present date regarding the broken map data, API error resolution, or portal maintenance for the 2024 and 2025 elections.

Statutory Compliance:
If you determine that any portion of these requested records is exempt from disclosure under Chapter 119, Florida law requires you to state in writing the basis for the exemption, including the exact statutory citation authorizing the redaction or denial.

Please advise me of any costs associated with fulfilling this request prior to processing.

Thank you for your lawful compliance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Matthew Joseph Luecht

communications.subject: RE: Florida Sunshine Law Request: EscambiaVotes GIS Mapping Data & IT Administration (2022-Present)
communications.communication:
To the Public Records Custodian:

On March 31, 2026, a highly specific public records request was submitted to your office pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law (Chapter 119, F.S.). As of today, April 9, 2026, I have received no acknowledgment of receipt, no status update, and no indication of any costs associated with fulfilling this request.

While Chapter 119 permits agencies a "reasonable time" to compile records, the complete failure to even acknowledge a legally submitted request after seven business days falls outside the statutory expectation of a good-faith response.

If your office intends to claim that the requested administrative records, IT logs, or vendor communications are exempt from public disclosure, Florida law requires you to state that exemption in writing, including the specific statutory citation.

Please immediately confirm receipt of the original March 31 request and provide an estimated timeline for the production of the records or the statutory basis for their denial. Continued silence will be documented as a refusal to comply with Chapter 119.

communications.subject: RE: Florida Sunshine Law Request #26-32
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Crawley,

Thank you for your response on April 23, 2026, and for providing the email record from VR Systems.

The document provided is an automated receipt confirming the creation of VR Systems support Ticket #: 53238. While I understand Mr. Grimes may not have initiated further outbound emails to the vendor, the creation of this ticket strongly indicates that additional records regarding the diagnosis and resolution of this issue exist within the agency's possession, whether generated internally or supplied by the vendor.

To ensure full compliance with Chapter 119, Florida Statutes, please consider this a follow-up public records request. I am requesting to inspect or obtain copies of the following:


Ticket Resolution Records: Any and all subsequent emails, vendor portal logs, status updates, or closing notes provided by VR Systems to Escambia County regarding the diagnosis, troubleshooting, and resolution of Ticket #: 53238.


Internal Communications: All internal emails, memorandums, or messages (including text messages or digital chat platforms like MS Teams) sent or received by Rusty Grimes or other SOE staff between May 6, 2025, and May 14, 2025, that reference the precinct mapping issue, the display of election results, or VR Systems.


Incident Documentation: Any IT incident reports, summaries, or documentation detailing the root cause of the precincts failing to display election results and the steps taken to restore the system.

As a reminder, under Florida law, the definition of a public record encompasses all materials made or received by an agency in connection with official business, regardless of the physical form or means of transmission.

If any part of this request is denied, or if any records are redacted, please explicitly cite the statutory exemption authorizing the withholding of the information.

Thank you for your time and your continued assistance with this matter.

title: FWS Space X Plover

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FWS Space X Plover
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the "2025 plover mutlivariable stats analysis", as referenced in the attached document from USFWS Region 4 staff. I also seek all USFWS reports from years 2024 and 2025 concerning the impacts to plovers from SpaceX operations in Florida, including reports referencing take, habitat degradation, population trends, etc. 

I am a reporter for Public Domain and other outlets.  I seek a fee waiver but will pay $25 if that waiver is not forthcoming.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jimmy Tobias

communications.subject: FOIA Request Acknowledgement: DOI-2025-009451
communications.communication:
The United States Fish and Wildlife Service (FWS) FOIA office received your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request dated September 11, 2025, and assigned it control number DOI-2025-009451. Please cite this number in any future communications with our office regarding your request for:

A copy of the "2025 plover mutlivariable stats analysis", as referenced in the attached document from USFWS Region 4 staff. I also seek all USFWS reports from years 2024 and 2025 concerning the impacts to plovers from SpaceX operations in Florida, including reports referencing take, habitat degradation, population trends, etc.

The FOIA provides for the collection of fees based on the costs of processing a FOIA request and your fee category. We have classified you as a representative of the news media and you have asked us to waive the fees for processing of your request. In this case, we have determined that there is no billable cost to process your request, therefore, there is no need to address your fee waiver request.

We use Multitrack Processing to process FOIA requests. The Simple track is for requests that would generally take one to five workdays to process. The Normal track is for requests that would generally take six to twenty workdays to process. The Complex track is for requests that would generally take twenty-one to sixty workdays to process. The Extraordinary track is for requests that would generally take more than sixty workdays to process. The Expedited track is for requests that have been granted expedited processing, which are processed as soon as practicable. Within each track, requests are usually processed on a first-in, first-out basis. Your request falls into the Normal processing track. There are 198 requests pending ahead of yours in this processing track. Presuming that our current FOIA capacity and burden remain constant, we expect that we will dispatch a determination to you by October 9, 2025.

If you have any questions, please feel free to contact us at fwhq_foia@fws.gov<mailto:fwhq_foia@fws.gov>.

Sincerely,

Matthew Sullivan
Government Information Specialist
U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service

communications.subject: FOIA Request Acknowledgement: DOI-2025-009451
communications.communication:
The United States Fish and Wildlife Service (FWS) FOIA office received your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request dated September 12, 2025, and assigned it control number DOI-2025-009478. Please cite this number in any future communications with our office regarding your request for:

All emails sent or received by FWS biologist Sarah Termondt that contain the following keyword: "SpaceX". This request seeks records produced between May 1, 2024 and May 1, 2025.

The FOIA provides for the collection of fees based on the costs of processing a FOIA request and your fee category. We have classified you as a representative of the news media and you have asked us to waive the fees for processing your request. In this case, we have determined that there is no billable cost to process your request, therefore, there is no need to address your fee waiver request.

We use Multitrack Processing to process FOIA requests. The Simple track is for requests that would generally take one to five workdays to process. The Normal track is for requests that would generally take six to twenty workdays to process. The Complex track is for requests that would generally take twenty-one to sixty workdays to process. The Extraordinary track is for requests that would generally take more than sixty workdays to process. The Expedited track is for requests that have been granted expedited processing, which are processed as soon as practicable. Within each track, requests are usually processed on a first-in, first-out basis. Your request falls into the Normal processing track. There are 198 requests pending ahead of yours in this processing track. Presuming that our current FOIA capacity and burden remain constant, we expect that we will dispatch a determination to you by October 9, 2025.

We use Multitrack Processing to process FOIA requests. The Simple track is for requests that would generally take one to five workdays to process. The Normal track is for requests that would generally take six to twenty workdays to process. The Complex track is for requests that would generally take twenty-one to sixty workdays to process. The Extraordinary track is for requests that would generally take more than sixty workdays to process. The Expedited track is for requests that have been granted expedited processing, which are processed as soon as practicable. Within each track, requests are usually processed on a first-in, first-out basis. Because we will need to search for and collect requested records from field facilities or other units that are separate from the office processing the request, we have determined that your request falls into the Complex processing track and are taking a 10-workday extension under 43 C.F.R. § 2.19(b)<http://www.ecfr.gov/cgi-bin/text-idx?c=ecfr&sid=0fc3ab3499768eebc2e3691c8cf88dec&rgn=div5&view=text&node=43:1.1.1.1.2&idno=43#43:1.1.1.1.2.4.5.6>. There are 452 requests pending ahead of yours in this processing track. Presuming that our current FOIA capacity and burden remain constant, we expect that we will dispatch a determination to you by May 21, 2026.
We apologize for this delay in finalizing our decision. You may narrow the scope of your request to obtain quicker processing in your currently assigned track or move the request into a different track (which may have the effect of having it processed more quickly). You also may agree to an alternative time period for processing by communicating with us. If after contacting us at fwhq_foia@fws.gov,<mailto:fwhq_foia@fws.gov,> you need further information or assistance with your request, you may wish to seek dispute resolution services from the Department’s FOIA Public Liaison by email at doifoiapublicliaison@sol.doi.gov. <mailto:doifoiapublicliaison@sol.doi.gov.>

If you need further information or assistance after contacting the Department’s FOIA Public Liaison, you may wish to seek dispute resolution services from the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS). The 2007 FOIA amendments created the OGIS to offer mediation services to resolve disputes between FOIA requesters and Federal agencies as a non-exclusive alternative to litigation. Using OGIS services does not affect your right to pursue litigation. You may contact OGIS in any of the following ways:

Office of Government Information Services
National Archives and Records Administration
8601 Adelphi Road - OGIS
College Park, MD 20740-6001
E-mail: ogis@nara.gov<mailto:ogis@nara.gov>
Web: https://www.archives.gov/ogis
Telephone: 202-741-5770
Toll-free: 1-877-684-6448

Please note that using OGIS services does not affect the timing of filing an appeal with the Department’s FOIA & Privacy Act Appeals Officer.

You may file an appeal to the Department’s FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Officer regarding this response. If you choose to appeal, the FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Officer must receive your FOIA appeal no later than 90 workdays from the date of this communication. Appeals arriving or delivered after 5 p.m. Eastern Time, Monday through Friday, will be deemed received on the next workday.
Your appeal must be made in writing. You may submit your appeal and accompanying materials to the FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Officer by mail, courier service, fax, or email. All communications concerning your appeal should be clearly marked with the words: "FREEDOM OF INFORMATION APPEAL." You must include an explanation of why you believe this response is in error. You must also include with your appeal copies of all correspondence between you and FWS concerning your FOIA request, including your original FOIA request and this response. Failure to include with your appeal all correspondence between you and FWS will result in the Department's rejection of your appeal, unless the FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Officer determines (in the FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Officer’s sole discretion) that good cause exists to accept the defective appeal.
Please include your name and daytime telephone number (or the name and telephone number of an appropriate contact), email address and fax number (if available) in case the FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Officer needs additional information or clarification of your appeal.

DOI FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Office Contact Information
Department of the Interior
Office of the Solicitor
1849 C Street, N.W.
MS-6556 MIB
Washington, DC 20240
Attn: FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Office
Telephone: (202) 208-5339
Fax: (202) 208-6677
Email: FOIA.Appeals@sol.doi.gov<mailto:FOIA.Appeals@sol.doi.gov>

If you have any questions, please feel free to contact us at fwhq_foia@fws.gov<mailto:fwhq_foia@fws.gov>.

Sincerely,

Matthew Sullivan
Government Information Specialist
U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: 2026 Brady List (Whatcom County Prosecuting Attorney)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: R000017-010526
communications.communication:
Dear Rose Terse: 
Thank you for your interest in public records of Whatcom County. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. Your request was received in this office on 1/5/2026 and given the reference number R000017-010526 for tracking purposes. Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rose Terse 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2169962/ 
Not all public documents are available in electronic format. If the document(s) requested are not available electronically, we will make them available for inspection or by paper copy in accordance with the Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. 
 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. PLEASE NOTE: The State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW, does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. Again, thank you for using the Public Records Center. 
Whatcom County Public Records Officer

communications.subject: Whatcom County Public Records Request - R000017-010526
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

I'm in receipt of your request to resend the Brady list and accompanying PID materials for Whatcom County.  I am preparing a response now, and hope to have that out to you today.

The second part of your request asks for "accompanying potential impeachment material."  Will the accompanying PID letters with a brief explanation of the reasons for for addition to the list be sufficient?

My direct line is 360-778-5755 if you would like to discuss.

Best,


Megan K. Winter, Paralegal- Civil Division
Whatcom County Prosecuting Attorney
311 Grand Avenue, Suite 201
Bellingham, WA 98225
(360) 778-5710
MWinter@co.whatcom.wa.us<mailto:MWinter@co.whatcom.wa.us>

Please be advised that these communications may be subject to disclosure under RCW 42.56, the Washington State Public Records Act.

CONFIDENTIAL OR PRIVILEGED:  This communication is intended only for the use of the individuals to whom it is addressed and may contain information that is privileged, confidential or exempt from other disclosure under applicable law.  If you are not the intended recipient, you are notified that any disclosure, printing, copying, distribution or use of the contents is prohibited.  If you have received this in error, please notify the sender immediately by telephone or by returning it by reply e-mail and then permanently deleting the communication from your system. Thank you.

With the following attachments we consider this request for:
"All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material" closed.  

We have provided you with responsive records in our possession.  Because we do not intend to further address this request, the one-year statute of limitations for legal action has begun.  
Please feel free to reach out to the Whatcom County Public Records Officer @ PublicRecordsOfficer@co.whatcom.wa.us, if you have any questions or concerns.

title: REQUEST FOR RECORDS RICKEY LEE ZARSKY

communications.subject: Government Records Access and Management Act Request: REQUEST FOR RECORDS RICKEY LEE ZARSKY
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Government Records Access and Management Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting any records in the possesion of Weber County Sheriff's Office from 01/01/2025 through present date 04/17/2026  for Rickey Lee Zarsky DOB 11/22/1953

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Annemarie Grant

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] Government Records Access and Management Act Request: REQUEST FOR RECORDS RICKEY LEE ZARSKY
communications.communication:
"Hello Annemarie,

 

I have attached the records the Weber County Sheriff’s Office has for Rickey Lee Zarsky. We only had a booking sheet from 05-04-2025.

 

There were five records out of the Ogden police department:

25G32617

25G67744

25G63248

25G71193

25G6359

 

You can contact the Ogden Police Department at: https://ogdenut.govqa.us/WEBAPP/_rs/(S(tpc4tu2znlhz0uuclz4ctmkh))/supporthome.aspx

 

I have waived the fee for the booking sheet.

 

Thank you,

 

Jaimi Breckley

 

Lead Operations Clerk

Training Bureau/ Hiring

GRAMA"

title: Inmate hourly pay

communications.subject: Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act Request: Inmate hourly pay
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Documentation of inmate hourly pay and method of payment for vocational opportunities, including but not limited to: Barber, Commissary Worker, Commercial Cleaning, Serv-Safe Certification, Custodial Worker, General Maintenance Worker, Laundry Worker, Law Clerk, Library Clerk, Pod-Runner and Tutor. 
Documentation of employment rates and types of employment of ICE detainees in 2025 and 2026.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sophia Wright

title: Benedetto Protect the Harvest

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Benedetto Protect the Harvest
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any and all written or electronic communications, including attachments, sent or received by Kathleen Benedetto, or her executive staff, and any agent, representative or executive of the advocacy group Protect the Harvest (http://protecttheharvest.com/). This request includes any and all communications between Benedetto, or her executive assistants, and any email address that ends in "@protecttheharvest.com". This request includes any and all communications between  Benedetto, or his executive assistants, and Brian Klippenstein. This request seeks records produced between March 1, 2017 and the date this request is processed.

I am a reporter for Pacific Standard magazine.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jimmy Tobias

communications.subject: FOIA 2018-01066
communications.communication:
Good afternoon Mr. Tobias,

Our FOIA office received your FOIA request, dated July 12, 2018. We have
assigned it control number 2018-01066. Please cite this number in any
future communications with our office regarding your request.

In your request you asked for the following:

"Any and all written or electronic communications, including attachments,
sent or received by Kathleen Benedetto, or her executive staff, and any
agent, representative or executive of the advocacy group Protect the
Harvest (http://protecttheharvest.com/). This request includes any and all
communications between Benedetto, or her executive assistants, and any
email address that ends in "@protecttheharvest.com". This request includes
any and all communications between Benedetto, or his executive assistants,
and Brian Klippenstein. This request seeks records produced between March
1, 2017 and the date this request is processed."

We have classified you as a an "other-use" requester. As such, we
may charge you for some of our search and duplication costs, but we will
not charge you for our review costs; you are also entitled to up to 2 hours
of search time and 100 pages of photocopies (or an equivalent volume) for
free. See 43 C.F.R. § 2.39. If, after taking into consideration your
fee category
entitlements, our processing costs are less than $50.00, we will not bill
you because the cost of collection would be greater than the fee collected.
See 43 C.F.R. § 2.49(a)(1).

We use Multitrack Processing to process FOIA requests. The Simple track is
for requests that can be processed in one to five workdays. The
Normal track is for requests that can be processed in six to twenty
workdays. The Complex track is for requests that can be processed in
twenty-one to sixty workdays. The Exceptional/Voluminous track is for
requests requiring more than sixty workdays for processing. The
Expedited track is for requests that have been granted expedited
processing. Within each track, requests are usually processed on a
first-in, first-out basis.

This request falls into the *Normal* track.


[image: Inline image 1]
Washington Office FOIA Coordinators
Bureau of Land Management
Phone: 202-912-7650
Fax: 202-245-0027
*BLM_WO_FOIA@blm.gov <BLM_WO_FOIA@blm.gov>*

communications.subject: FOIA Online Request No. DOI-BLM-2018-000806
communications.communication:
On July 12, 2018, you made a FOIA request to BLM for:

From 3/1/17 to 7/12/18: all written or electronic communications, sent or received by Kathleen Benedetto, or her executive staff, and any agent, representative or executive of the advocacy group Protect the Harvest (http://protecttheharvest.com/).  any and all communications between Benedetto, or her executive assistants, & any email address that ends in @protecttheharvest.com". any and all communications between Benedetto, or his executive assistants, & Brian Klippenstein.


Currently the Bureau is inquiring to see if you have a continuing interest in receiving records responsive to this request.  We are attempting to best service all our requesters by working on cases for which there is an ongoing and continuing need.

If we do not receive your reply in the affirmative within 60 business days, the BLM will assume you are no longer interested in pursuing this request and we will administratively close it.


Thank you,

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Police disciplinary files (Jefferson County Prosecuting Attorney)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

- Any and all lists, spreadsheets or other organized datasets that detail potential or substantiated allegations of misconduct and/or impeached or impeachable testimony, created under Brady v. Maryland and/or Giglio v. United States.
- Any and all records regarding substantiated allegations of misconduct or violations of internal department policy or criminal law by specific law enforcement officers.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Moe Clark

communications.subject: [Records Center] County Public Records Request :: C002790-071325
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of July 13, 2025, Reference # C002790-071325 To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

1. Any and all lists, spreadsheets or other organized datasets that detail potential or substantiated allegations of misconduct and/or impeached or impeachable testimony, created under Brady v. Maryland and/or Giglio v. United States. 

2. Any and all records regarding substantiated allegations of misconduct or violations of internal department policy or criminal law by specific law enforcement officers. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 
Dear Moe  Clark, We have provided responsive records for part 1 of your request. Before we can proceed with part 2, it would be good to obtain clarification from you regarding part 2 which is an extremely broad request. You have not provided a date range, so as we read your request, we infer it to mean that you want every single records pertaining to every incident and investigation of any policy violation, regardless of how long ago it occurred. Are we interpreting this correctly? If so, then this will entail a time-consuming and expensive process.  Most of the records involved exist only in paper form, so they would need to be scanned in order to produce them to you. The county charges a scanning fee of 10 cents per page, authorized by RCW 42.56.120(2)(a)(ii). Given the large total number of pages involved, it's not hard to estimate that the scanning fees will run well into hundreds of dollars, perhaps over a thousand. In addition, it will necessarily take many months to produce all such documents for two reasons: 1. All the records would need to be carefully reviewed for potential exemptions from disclosure. 2. Prior to release of each investigation, the county is required, per RCW 42.56.250(2), to send notice to the subject of the investigation regarding the records request, and then allow them opportunity (around 10 to 14 days) to file a petition in court to enjoin release of the records. Given all of this, we are wondering if it would suit your purposes to narrow your request in some way. We have provided you with the county's Brady list as well as the county sheriff's internal affairs log. Between the two, they list all allegations of misconduct and policy or criminal violations by sheriff employees, back through 2015. It may be helpful for you to have a look at the documents we provided and narrow your request to specific cases that are most interesting or relevant to you. Please advise. We will await your response before proceeding further with your request. Sincerely, Ken Hugoniot 
Public Records Administrator 
Central Services 
Jefferson County 
360-385-9174

Some additional time will be needed to locate, compile and review responsive records. In order to allow time for legally-mandated third party notice to be given to employees who are the subjects of the records, w e estimate that the next installment of public records responsive to this request will be available within ten business days from today, on or before 8/29/2025. If public records become available in advance of this estimate we will contact you promptly. Conversely, if the County encounters a need to extend our estimate, we will contact you with a revised estimated date. Thank you for your patience in awaiting this information. Sincerely, Ken Hugoniot

2. Any and all records regarding substantiated allegations of misconduct or violations of internal department policy or criminal law by specific law enforcement officers that appear on Jefferson County's Brady List or PID List. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Moe Clark 

Upload documents directly: https://accounts.muckrock.com/accounts/login/?next=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.muckrock.com%2Faccounts%2Flogin%2F%3Fnext%3D%252Faccounts%252Fagency_login%252Fjefferson-county-prosecuting-attorney-35780%252Fpolice-disciplinary-files-jefferson-county-prosecuting-attorney-189443%252F%253Femail%253Djkennedy%252540co.jefferson.wa.us&url_auth_token=AAGwWgnpEdISqOKXq_SEAdaKa9Y%3A1ub9rJ%3AeCqK8v0pjBx5tS_qnt8lNlK-7w0h4Q4HhQMxxoz2EgA 
The second installment of records responsive to your request is available to you now in the Jefferson County Public Records Center. These are the PID letters that correspond to the five Jefferson County Sheriff officers that are included in the PID list that we provided to you. Subsequent installments will consist of the reports of the investigations which resulted in the potential impeachment disclosure for each of the officers. Please log in at the following link to retrieve your responsive records: County Public Records Request - C002790-071325  It has taken longer than expected to compile and review the next installment of responsive records. The county is required to provide third party notice to employees who are the subjects of requests for records in personnel files. All legal review must be completed prior to sending third party notice so that the subjects of the records can adequately review the records that the county intends to provide to the requester. The county must also allow the subjects at least ten days to review the records and, if they so choose, to file a petition in court to enjoin release of the records. We hope to send out third party notice tomorrow for the next installments of records. We estimate that records will be available within ten business days from today, on or before 9/16/2025. Sincerely, Ken Hugoniot 
Public Records Administrator 
Central Services 
Jefferson County 
360-385-9174

communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of July 13, 2025, Reference # C002790-071325 To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 
1. Any and all lists, spreadsheets or other organized datasets that detail potential or substantiated allegations of misconduct and/or impeached or impeachable testimony, created under Brady v. Maryland and/or Giglio v. United States. 
2. Any and all records regarding substantiated allegations of misconduct or violations of internal department policy or criminal law by specific law enforcement officers that appear on Jefferson County's Brady List or PID List. 
Dear Moe  Clark, We have provided all of the reports and supporting documentation for all five of the Jefferson County Sheriff officers who appear on the PID list. The only other related records would be any emails that were produced during the investigations of these incidents. Do you want copies of those emails? If so, it will take a number of weeks, or a couple of months, to locate and compile those emails. We would provide them in additional installments. Please advise whether you want those email records. Sincerely, Ken Hugoniot 
Public Records Administrator 
Central Services 
Jefferson County 
360-385-9174

Thank you so much for responding to my records request. We can wait on the email correspondances for now, the PID letters should suffice. I appreciate your help on this request.

Best,

Moe

communications.subject: [Records Center] County Public Records Request :: C002790-071325
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of July 13, 2025, Reference # C002790-071325 Dear Moe Clark, Jefferson County received the following public records request from you on July 13, 2025: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

1. Any and all lists, spreadsheets or other organized datasets that detail potential or substantiated allegations of misconduct and/or impeached or impeachable testimony, created under Brady v. Maryland and/or Giglio v. United States. 

2. Any and all records regarding substantiated allegations of misconduct or violations of internal department policy or criminal law by specific law enforcement officers that appear on Jefferson County's Brady List or PID List. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Moe Clark 

Upload documents directly: https://accounts.muckrock.com/accounts/login/?next=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.muckrock.com%2Faccounts%2Flogin%2F%3Fnext%3D%252Faccounts%252Fagency_login%252Fjefferson-county-prosecuting-attorney-35780%252Fpolice-disciplinary-files-jefferson-county-prosecuting-attorney-189443%252F%253Femail%253Djkennedy%252540co.jefferson.wa.us&url_auth_token=AAGwWgnpEdISqOKXq_SEAdaKa9Y%3A1ub9rJ%3AeCqK8v0pjBx5tS_qnt8lNlK-7w0h4Q4HhQMxxoz2EgA 
In accordance with your communication to us, Jefferson County has closed this records request. This is the final, definitive response from Jefferson County to your request. This response completes and closes out Jefferson County Public Records Request #C002790-071325 Jefferson County will take no further action on this request. Pursuant to RCW 42.56.550, y ou may seek judicial review of the county's response to your request for a period of one year from the date of this message. 
If you have any questions or issues regarding any aspect of the county's response to your request, or the records provided to you, please respond directly to this email or contact the Jefferson County Public Records Administrator within twenty (20) days of the date of this message. If you believe that the county has failed to provide any responsive records, either by withholding or omission, you may request a review of the county's response by the Prosecuting Attorney's Office (PAO), pursuant to Section 9.0 of the Jefferson County Public Records Act Compliance Policy. To request a PAO review, please respond to this email accordingly, or notify the Jefferson County Public Records Administrator within twenty (20) days of the date of this message. Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

1) Copy of the signed and executed contract between D86 and Gary Grasso dba Grasso Law regarding hiring for legal services including the hourly rate; 
2) Copies of all documents between Grasso/his law firm AND D86’s law firm of Odelson, Murphey, Frazier & McGrath AND D86 regarding hiring (or potential to hire) the law firm of Grasso Law 
3) Copies of all documents between and among D86 officials regarding the hiring, or potential to hire, the Grasso Law firm 

for the period August 1, 2025 through April 1, 2026 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Anonymous User 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2260013/ 
 Under FOIA, a public body may extend the time to respond to a FOIA request by up to 5 business days for a limited number of reasons. 5 ILCS 140/3(e). We are extending the time to respond to your request by 5 business days for the following reasons: 
  
  
  
  
 The requested records require examination and evaluation by personnel having the necessary competence and discretion to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of this Act or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
 The request for records cannot be complied with by the District within the initial five business days allowed under FOIA without unduly burdening or interfering with the District’s operations. 
  
 FOIA Officer

--- Please respond above this line --- 
April 22, 2026 
Anonymous User 
FOIA Request # R001627-040826 
Hinsdale Township High School District 86 received your request for information pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (“FOIA”) 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. and subsequently extended the time to respond as allowed under FOIA 5 ILCS 140/3(e) for the following reasons: 

 
  *  
 The requested records require examination and evaluation by personnel having the necessary competence and discretion to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of this Act or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 

 
  *  
 The request for records cannot be complied with by the District within the initial five business days allowed under FOIA without unduly burdening or interfering with the District’s operations. 
The district now provides its timely response for your request for the following records: 
1) Copy of the signed and executed contract between D86 and Gary Grasso dba Grasso Law regarding hiring for legal services including the hourly rate; 
2) Copies of all documents between Grasso/his law firm AND D86’s law firm of Odelson, Murphey, Frazier & McGrath AND D86 regarding hiring (or potential to hire) the law firm of Grasso Law 
3) Copies of all documents between and among D86 officials regarding the hiring, or potential to hire, the Grasso Law firm 
for the period August 1, 2025 through April 1, 2026 
Your request is granted in part and denied in part. Records responsive to your request are enclosed via the Public Records Center . Information contained within these records has been redacted pursuant to the below provisions of the Illinois Freedom of Information Act: 
 
  *  
 Section 7(1)(c) Personal information, the disclosure of which would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy, unless the disclosure is consented to in writing by the individual subjects of the information. 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(c). 

 
  *  
 Section 7(1)(m) Communications between a public body and an attorney or auditor representing the public body that would not be subject to discovery in litigation, and materials prepared or compiled by or for a public body in anticipation of a criminal, civil or administrative proceeding. 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(m). 
As to this partial denial, you are advised that you have the right to a review of this decision by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Attorney General. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). You can file your Request for Review with the PAC by writing to: 
Public Access Counselor Office of the Attorney General 500 South 2nd Street Springfield, Illinois 62706 1-877-299-FOIA 1-877-299-3642 Public.Access@ilag.gov 
If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 days of the date of this letter. 5 ILCS 140/9/5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. 
You also have the right to seek judicial review of any denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court. 5 ILCS 140/11.  As the District’s FOIA Officer, I am responsible for the District’s response to your FOIA request. This letter and enclosures are intended to be fully responsive to your specific request. The district's response has been prepared in accordance with our FOIA Operational Calendar and 2:250 AP1 (Access to and Copying of District Public Records) .  If I have misunderstood your request in any way, please contact me in writing.  This completes your request for records.  Debra Kedrowski FOIA Officer Hinsdale Township High School District 86 5500 South Grant Street - Hinsdale IL 60521 630.655.6108 
 dkedrows@hinsdale86.org

title: Data Breach Notification Records Maintained by This Office

communications.subject: Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act Request: Data Breach Notification Records Maintained by This Office
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request records tracking all data breach notifications received by this office from January 1, 2018 to the present, in whatever format such records are currently maintained (e.g. database, spreadsheet, or log). Please provide all fields pertaining to each breach notification contained in your existing records.

By way of example, information of interest includes: notifying entity name and type, date notification was received, date of breach or discovery, type of personal information affected, number of state residents affected, and nature or cause of the breach.

Please provide responsive records in electronic format (CSV or Excel preferred over PDF where possible).

I request a waiver of any applicable fees. I am an assistant professor at the University of New Hampshire conducting academic research on data breach patterns and consumer protection for the public benefit. This request is not for commercial use. If a full waiver is not granted, please notify me before incurring costs exceeding $50.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Karl Grindal

title: Foul Play In Workplace Report

communications.subject: Nevada Public Records Act Request: Foul Play In Workplace Report
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any documentation in regards to Your Choice Home Health's (1135 Terminal Way Ste 208, Reno Nevada 89502) clientele relating to foul play between staff and patients during work hours between the dates of January 1st, 2025 through December 31st, 2025.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ricky Padilla

Dear Ricky Padilla: 
Your request has been received and is being processed.  Your request was received by the Nevada Department of Human Services on 3/1/2026 and given the reference number R006708-030126 for tracking purposes. Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Any documentation in regards to Your Choice Home Health's (1135 Terminal Way Ste 208, Reno Nevada 89502) clientele relating to foul play between staff and patients during work hours between the dates of January 1st, 2025 through December 31st, 2025. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Ricky Padilla 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2229817/ 
Your request will be forwarded to the appropriate staff to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question.

Nevada Department of Human Services

communications.subject: [Records Center] Public Records Request :: R006708-030126
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of March 01, 2026, Reference # R006708-030126 
Dear Ricky Padilla, 
The Nevada Department of Human Services received a public information request from you on March 01, 2026. Your request mentioned (and clarification received, if any): 
“Any documentation in regards to Your Choice Home Health's (1135 Terminal Way Ste 208, Reno Nevada 89502) clientele relating to foul play between staff and patients during work hours between the dates of January 1st, 2025 through December 31st, 2025." 

Health facility licensing by the Bureau of Health Care Quality and Compliance has been transferred from the Nevada Department of Human Services (DHS) to the Nevada Health Authority (NVHA). Your request can be submitted to NVHA by sending an email to nvhapio@nvha.nv.gov. (mailto:nvhapio@nvha.nv.gov) 
For DHS, this request is considered complete; further inquiries can be made by submitting a new public records request with the Nevada Department of Human Services. 
Sincerely, 
Nathan Orme 
Public Information Officer 
Director's Office

communications.subject: RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of March 2, 2025., Reference # 018
communications.communication:
Dear Requestor,

The Nevada Health Authority received a public records request from you on March 2, 2025. Your request mentioned:

"Any documentation in regards to Your Choice Home Health's (1135 Terminal Way Ste 208, Reno Nevada 89502) clientele relating to foul play between staff and patients during work hours between the dates of January 1st, 2025 through December 31st, 2025."
The Department will conduct a review of records in our custody to determine if any responsive records exist. Further, the Department will need to review any responsive records to redact any confidential or privileged information prior to providing those records. The Department is currently experiencing a high volume of public information requests, and our office anticipates responding to this request by 4/1/2026.

If you have any questions, please contact me by replying to this message. Thank you for your attention.

Sincerely,

Claire Walton
Education and Information Officer
[Logo Nevada Health Authority Ensuring affordable, reliable health care for Nevada]

communications.communication:
To whom it may concern,

I am still awaiting a response to this matter and would like to clarify the objectives I am seeking redirect my request to more specific documentation. I no longer want "foul play" among staff and I would like to ask to inspection reports, complaint investigations, deficiency citations during the same timeframes mentioned previously (January 1st, 2025 through December 31st, 2025). I am still asking for the request to relate with Your Choice Home Health's (1135 Terminal Way Ste 208, Reno Nevada 89502).

Sincerely,

Ricky Padilla

communications.subject: RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of March 2, 2025., Reference # 018
communications.communication:
Good morning,

Thank you for your patience on a response to your request. We identified three complaints within the timeframe you provided (January 1, 2025, through December 31, 2025). However, these complaints may not directly relate to your request for: “Any documentation in regards to Your Choice Home Health's (1135 Terminal Way Ste 208, Reno, Nevada 89502) clientele relating to foul play between staff and patients during work hours.”
We have attached the survey report associated with the three mentioned complaints received during the referenced timeframe. Please review the attached report.

Let me know if you have any questions!

Claire Walton
Education and Information Officer - HCQC
Nevada Health Authority
Health Care Purchasing and Compliance Division
775-684-1070 | cwalton@nvha.nv.gov<mailto:cwalton@nvha.nv.gov>

title: Thomas Gregory

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Thomas Gregory
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records created by Thomas Gregory using Large Language Models "LLMs" including but not exclusive to Chat GPT, Claude, Deepseek, Google Gemini, and all others between May 1st, 2023 until April 10 2026. 

These documents should include screen shots of chat logs and project folders of any LLM Thomas Gregory uses for his job with the City of Berkeley.

Any documents receipt for LLM's created by City of Berkeley or Thomas Gregory. 

All policies and procedures by the City of Berkeley using LLM's .

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robbie Powelson

title: FOIA - Michael A. Taylor Incident

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: FOIA - Michael A. Taylor Incident
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

An electronic copy of the bodycam footage regarding the Michael A. Taylor incident that occurred on October 22, 2024 after a traffic stop at the Dollar General located at 914 Sheridan Rd, Winthrop Harbor, IL 60096. Taylor was arrested on the charges of bribery, aggravated battery to a peace officer and resisting a peace officer causing injury.

Specifically, I am seeking to obtain electronic copies of the:

1. BWC footage recorded by Winthrop Harbor Police Department's responding officers (main officer and assisting) detailing their full involvement in this incident

To assist you in locating the footage here are the key details of the incident:
Date of Event: October 22, 2024
Names: Michael A. Taylor
Location of Event: 914 Sheridan Rd, Winthrop Harbor, IL 60096

Please let me know if you require further information to process it.

If any aspects of this request are denied, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and please notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John Johnson

title: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Barrien County)

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Barrien County)
communications.communication:
Dear Berrien County Administration FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the Berrien County Administration county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

communications.subject: FW: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Barrien County)
communications.communication:
Hello,

Your request was forwarded to me.  The 2026 county-wide assessment roll will be available towards the end of the month.  The cost for the files will be $2,000 which is the max charge.  You will receive the values, name/address, legal description, and sales file.  I have attached the file layout so you can see what fields are included.  This is the same export that I run for Zillow and others.  I would send it to you through ShareFile where you will then be able to download the files.



If this is something that you would like to purchase, I will need the name, address and phone number to use for the invoice.   The invoice will need to be paid before the files are sent so if you can let me know as soon as possible, I would appreciate it.
Thanks,

Dawn Marquardt
Information Systems
(269) 983-7111 x8428
dmarquar@berriencounty.org

files.title: image001
files.source: Berrien County Administration, Berrien County, MI

files.title: valuesexport
files.source: Berrien County Administration, Berrien County, MI

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Barrien County)
communications.communication:
Hi Dawn,

Would it be possible for you to forward me a copy of an earlier export which was already provided to Zillow or other data brokers?

It would most likely contain the same information, and I was thinking if eg Zillow's export existed in an email sent box or a folder somewhere, that could save you the time/work of having to create a new export.

The reason I ask is that $2000 is above what the FOIA would normally provide for (which I believe bases its cost in terms of county's hours in labor, etc)-- so if you could share the same public records provided to Zillow under FOIA's fee structure, I would really appreciate it (and am willing to pay fees under FOIA).

Feel free to call if it's easier, 734-272-6612

Thank you again for your help with this.

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Barrien County)
communications.communication:
Hello,
I do not keep the files that I send them because the information is constantly changing with names/addresses and sales and I don’t want to accidentally send the wrong file.  We bill under the TARA act of .30/parcel with a max of $2,000.  There are well over 90,000 parcels in the file.

Dawn Marquardt
Information Systems
(269) 983-7111 x8428
dmarquar@berriencounty.org

Could you give me the MCL# for the TARA act?

title: 2026 Brady List (Mason County Prosecuting Attorney)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: 2026 Brady List (Mason County Prosecuting Attorney)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

communications.subject: Mason County Records Request :: C020489-041526
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

Hi Rose, 
We received a similar request in July 2025 from your organization; we’ve only added two names and two documents. May we send a complete updated list and the two additional letters that go along with the new names?  You will then have a complete copy of all that you have requested in an expedited manner. 
Thank you, 
Kelsey

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #C020489-041526
communications.communication:
Hi Kelsey,

MuckRock (www.muckrock.com) is actually a platform that facilitates submitting and tracking records requests for its users, so I'm not familiar with the other user who submitted the previous request. Would you mind sending the list and all letters (excluding those for WSP officers)?

Thank you,
Rose

Date: 04/22/2026 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST #C020489-041526 
Dear Rose , 
This letter is in regards to your Public Records Request dated on April 15, 2026, for the following: 
 
  * “To Whom It May Concern: 
 
Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 
 
All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material. 
 
The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 
 
In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 
 
Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 
 
Sincerely, 
 
Rose Terse 
 
Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2265049/” This letter is to notify you that Mason County has completed its search for responsive records regarding your Public Records Request and all responsive records have been provided.  Driver's license numbers have been redacted pursuant to RCW 42.56.230(5).  This request is now considered closed.  Pursuant to RCW 42.56.550(6), you have one year from the date of this letter to seek judicial review of this request.  If you have any questions pertaining to this request, please respond within twenty (20) days of this letter. 
Please see below for a link to the Public Records Portal to download your records. Mason County Records Request - C020489-041526 
Sincerely, 
Kelsey Crane 
Administrative Secretary

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Placer County Sheriff)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Placer County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Carville Ave 2020 Call Reports/Logs

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Carville Ave 2020 Call Reports/Logs
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Could I please obtain a copy of any call logs or reports from calls on Carville Ave throughout the year of 2020.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett

We are unable to send out exact reports without them being in a PDF File format. We do not have text to speech technology. Attached is a log in an excel format for the time line of January 1, 2020-December 31, 2020-as you requested not to send physical documents.

Thank you,
Lexington Police Incident Records Department
1575 Massachusetts Ave.
Lexington, MA 02420
781-862-1212

________________________________

When writing or responding, please be aware that the Massachusetts Secretary of State has determined that most email is a public record and, therefore, may not be kept confidential.

title: Leadership and constituent emails (Salem Clerk)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Salem Clerk)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by the chair of the select board related to their elected position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on personal and secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)
