title: Records Pertaining to Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (“KB Reid”) (United States Department of the Army)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Records Pertaining to Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (“KB Reid”) (United States Department of the Army)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

This request seeks all records from 1995 (or earlier) to 2010 (or later if available) referencing Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (also known as “KB Reid”), a U.S. Army Military Intelligence officer who was selected for the Military Intelligence Excepted Career Program (GREAT SKILL) in 1987 and subsequently served in some of the most sensitive Army and interagency HUMINT roles of the post–Cold War and post-9/11 era.

I respectfully request:

	1.	Personnel Records
	•	Official Military Personnel File (OMPF), Officer Evaluation Reports (OERs), commendations, promotions, awards, fitness reports, assignment orders, and internal performance evaluations for Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (SSN/Service Number can be provided if required).
	•	Decorations including: Defense Superior Service Medal, Bronze Star (with Oak Leaf Cluster), Defense Meritorious Service Medal (x3), Meritorious Service Medal (x3), Joint Service Commendation Medal, Army Commendation Medal (with Oak Leaf Cluster), Army Achievement Medal, Ranger tab, Special Forces tab, Pathfinder, Master Parachutist, and Military Free Fall Jump Master badges ￼.
	2.	Program and Command Assignments
	•	All records documenting his service in the GREAT SKILL Program, including appointment, reassignment memos, or internal assessments.
	•	His service as Regional Desk Officer and Special Assistant for Military Affairs at a national-level intelligence agency, with a focus on East Asian and Near East operations.
	•	Records from his time as Commander of a Defense HUMINT Service base in Bosnia, and as CJ2 for Combined Joint Task Force Fervent Archer.
	•	Documentation from his command of a sensitive HUMINT collection detachment deployed to Afghanistan (2002), where he served as the first HUMINT case officer supporting U.S. Special Operations Command (USSOCOM).
	•	Records from his second Afghanistan deployment (2004), where he commanded a HUMINT base supporting surge operations during Afghanistan’s first democratic elections.
	•	Internal records, rosters, orders, or awards tied to his 2005 Iraq deployment as a Senior HUMINT Advisor in a sensitive interagency coordination cell.
	3.	Strategic Instruction & Final Command
	•	Records of his time as Chief of the Military Group and Senior DoD Instructor at a joint interagency strategic intelligence training institution (likely DIA or a multi-agency DoD school).
	•	Materials from his command of the U.S. Army Field Support Center (AFSC) beginning in May 2009, including speeches, directives, or internal comms.
	•	Any documentation of his death from pancreatic cancer in 2010, honors received, or correspondence related to his funeral and burial at Arlington National Cemetery on September 9, 2010.
	4.	Legacy Sites and Honors
	•	Records regarding the 2011 naming of the DIA Contingency Operating Base in Kabul as Camp K.B. Reid, including naming justification, interagency approvals, and dedication ceremony photos or memos.
	•	Materials related to the Reid Center (DIA’s training facility in Norfolk, VA, established in 2012 and now closed).
	•	The establishment of the COL Kurush Bharucha-Reid Award for Excellence in HUMINT Tradecraft in 2014 by the HUMINT Training – Joint Center of Excellence, including award criteria, selection board minutes, and awardee names.
	•	Any correspondence, ceremony documentation, or Hall of Fame entry details from his 2014 induction into the Military Intelligence Corps Hall of Fame ￼.
	5.	Photographic and Ceremonial Records
	•	All official photographs, eulogies, obituaries, citations, press releases, internal honors, or other agency acknowledgments related to Col. Bharucha-Reid, both during service and posthumously.
	6.	Classified and Segregable Records
	•	If any records are classified, please initiate a mandatory declassification review (MDR) under Executive Order 13526 and release all segregable non-exempt portions under FOIA guidelines.
	7.	Delivery and Fee Waiver
	•	Please provide documents in searchable PDF format via secure email or download link.

This request supports a public-interest historical and educational project—specifically, a feature film titled Undercover Patriot exploring the intersection of identity, secrecy, and service. 

As a fellow Parsi Zoroastrian American, I believe Col. Bharucha-Reid’s legacy deserves recognition beyond the classified corridors in which he served.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Adil Minocherhomjee

The Washington State Military Department received a public information request from you on March 15, 2026. Your request mentioned:
 
“Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

On March 15, this recording of a presentation alleged to be given by Washington Emergency Management Division employee Caroline Byrd in her official capacity was posted to social media: https://www.instagram.com/stopsurveillancecity/reel/DV6k4SUD4v2/

Please provide all slide decks and recordings for this presentation. Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2240892/”
 
Thank you for your patience; I was delayed in providing these records to you yesterday due to an unexpected doctor appointment. Please see below for the records you seek;  note the video in question was not sourced from the WA Military Department.
 
The Washington State Military Department has reviewed its files and has located responsive records to your request. Under the authority of RCW 42.56.120(2)(b) and WAC 323-10-070, the Department now charges fees for providing copies of public records.

The cost for these records is as follows:

Fees Charged: Five cents per each four electronic files or attachment uploaded to email, cloud-based data storage service, or other means of electronic delivery.

The Department has estimated that the the expenses of processing payment exceeds the costs of providing these records. Therefore the fees for these records have been waived.

Please log in to the Public Records Portal at the following link to retrieve the appropriate responsive documents. 
 
Public Records Request - P001870-031526
 
 
This production of records is a complete response to your request. Your request has been fulfilled by the production of records (including any exemptions claimed) and is now closed. This is our final response. The WA Military Department does not intend to further address the request, and the PRA's one-year statute of limitations to seek judicial review has started to run as of the date of this email. Please contact me within 30 days by e-mail at public.records@mil.wa.gov or at Building One, 1 Militia Drive, Camp Murray, WA 98430-5000, if you have any questions.
 
Sincerely,
 

Cynthia Whaley
Public Records Officer 
Office of Intergovernmental Affairs & Policy

title: Flock Safety search audits, network sharing, and general order (Lynn Police Department)

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Flock Safety search audits, network sharing, and general order (Lynn Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Records related to the department’s Flock Safety system:
1. Organization Audit in Flock. 
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. A list of network share settings that include:
3a. Flock networks shared with me
3b. Networks that I’m sharing

Please provide records in their original electronic, machine readable format. For example, please provide csv or spreadsheet files where applicable. Please do not redirect me to your transparency portal.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robin Monks

title: DOJ Voter Data Agreements (Connecticut Secretary of State, Legislation and Elections Administration Division)

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Agreements (Connecticut Secretary of State, Legislation and Elections Administration Division)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Any and all memoranda of understanding (MOUs), agreements, contracts, or similar instruments between the state and the U.S. Department of Justice relating to the provision of voter registration data, voter files, or voter roll information, from January 20, 2025 to the present.

2. Any draft, unsigned, proposed, or rejected versions of such agreements.

3. Any amendments, addenda, or modifications to such agreements.

This request includes but is not limited to any document described or labeled as a "memorandum of understanding" or contract relating to voter data, voter list maintenance, or voter roll verification.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: PRR - Monica Hightower Incident
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Regarding the incident of Monica Hightower, DOB 03/26/1988, that occurred on 01/12/2024 at 2701 AVENUE E #15, RIVIERA BEACH, FL 33404. The suspect was arrested on the charges of child abuse with great bodily harm.
Specifically, I am seeking to obtain an electronic copy of the:
1. BWC footage recorded by Officer Lamn #7159 who responded to the incident detailing his/her full involvement in the incident.
To assist you in locating the footage here are the key details of the incident:
Agency Report Number: 23-00245
Date of Event: 01/12/2024   
Names: Monica Hightower
Location of Event: 2701 AVENUE E #15, RIVIERA BEACH, FL 33404
Please let me know if you require further information to process it.
If any aspects of this request are denied, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and please notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

title: Dataset: 911 Call Data (Communications Division, Stockton Fire Department, Stockton, Ca, Usa)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

RAW Computer Aided Dispatch Data queried out of your emergency phone system on 911 calls for service from January 2014 to the most recent date data is collected. In CSV, TXT, or XML format, a machine-readable spreadsheet (not XLSX format). 

Here are the column headers which I am requesting:

Master Incident Number or Event Number – Unique numerical identifier of the call for service
Case Number – If an incident report was written, this is the file number, which can be joined to the incidents and arrests datasets
Call Date – Date the call for service was generated
Call Time – Time the call for service was generated
Source – How the call was generated from the public, or self-initiated by an officer
Priority – The priority level of the call response, with ‘P’ being the highest, followed in order numerically
Nature – The description of the call for service.
Address – The block number and street or intersection of the call for service.
Latitude/longitude - coordinates for call of service
X – Mapping coordinate of the call for service
Y – Mapping coordinate of the call for service
District – The patrol district where the call for service was located.
Beat – The patrol beat where the call for service was located, which is a sub-division of the district
Tract – The census tract where the incident occurred, based on 2010 census data
Disposition – How the call for service was closed
Cancelled – Whether the call for service was cancelled prior to officer arrival
Dispatch Date and Time - The exact date and time when the dispatcher sent the call to emergency responders, such as police, fire, or EMS
Entry Date and Time - The date and time when the call was initially logged into the 911 computer-aided dispatch (CAD) system
Received Date and Time - The date and time when the 911 call was first answered by the dispatch center
Enroute Date and Time - The date and time when the assigned responder (e.g., a patrol car, ambulance, or fire truck) began traveling to the incident location
On Scene Date and Time - The date and time when the responder arrived at the scene of the incident
Close Date and Time - The date and time when the incident or call was marked as completed or resolved in the CAD system
Dispatcher ID - A unique identifier for the dispatcher who handled and managed the 911 call. This may be used for tracking or auditing purposes

If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Filed Requests

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Dataset: 911 Call Data (Communications Division, Stockton Fire Department, Stockton, Ca, Usa)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Upon review, additional data fields are necessary to fully interpret the records already requested and/or produced. I therefore request the following additional column headers, to the extent they exist in your CAD system, for the same date range (January 2014 to the most recent date data is collected), in CSV, TXT, or XML format:

* Unique Identifier – A unique numerical identifier for each call for service, if distinct from the master incident or event number
* Dispatcher ID(s) – Identifier(s) for all dispatchers who handled the call, if multiple
* Responder ID – A unique identifier for the responding unit(s) or team(s)
* Prime Unit – Identifier indicating the primary response unit
* Other Units – All additional responding unit identifiers
* Back-up Requested – Indicator or code showing whether back-up was requested
* Transport – Indicator (Y/N) of whether transport occurred
* Transport Start and End Times – If transport occurred
* Transport Destination Address – The address or facility to which transport occurred
* Program or Response Type – Any field identifying non-police, co-response, or alternative response teams (including behavioral health, crisis, or care-based units), if applicable

If any of the above fields are stored under different names, codes, or tables, I request the functional equivalent fields.

I also request any available data dictionary or codebook necessary to interpret these fields.

If you deny any portion of this supplemental request, please cite the specific statutory exemption relied upon and provide the applicable appeal procedures as required by law.

As before, these records are requested for non-commercial purposes and will be made available to the general public. Please inform me in advance of any fees associated with fulfilling this request.

Thank you for your cooperation.

Sincerely,
FR

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Dataset: 911 Call Data (Communications Division, Stockton Fire Department, Stockton, Ca, Usa)
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Weiman / To Whom It May Concern:

Thank you for the production of 911 CAD data in response to our request. After reviewing the produced file, we find that the production is substantially incomplete relative to what was requested. The file contains only the following fields:
CALL_CREATED_DATE, CALL_NO, ENTRY_OPER_ID, ENTRY_OPERATOR_NAME, DISPATCHER_ID, DISPATCHER_NAME, PRIMARY_UNIT, UNIT_ID, PERS_ID, PERS_NAME

The following fields were explicitly requested and are absent from the production. To the extent these fields exist in your CAD system — and we have good reason to believe many do — they are responsive and must either be produced or withheld with citation to a specific exemption under the CPRA:

Call characteristics:

Source (how the call was generated)
Priority level
Nature/call type description
Case Number (for linkage to incident and arrest datasets)
Disposition
Cancelled indicator

Geography:

Address or block-level location
Latitude/Longitude
X/Y mapping coordinates
District
Beat
Census Tract

Timestamps:

Received Date and Time
Dispatch Date and Time
Enroute Date and Time
On Scene Date and Time
Close Date and Time

Supplemental fields (requested 01/18/2026):

Back-up Requested indicator
Transport indicator (Y/N)
Transport Start and End Times
Transport Destination Address
Program or Response Type (including any field identifying alternative, co-response, or behavioral health response teams)

We also note that no codebook or data dictionary has been produced for this dataset.

The CPRA does not permit partial production without explanation. If any of the above fields are being withheld, please identify the specific statutory exemption justifying each withholding. If certain fields do not exist in your system, please state that explicitly for each field so that we can close out those items.

Please respond with either a supplemental production or a written exemption log within 10 calendar days.

Sincerely,
Filed Requests

title: Massachusetts Public Records Request - Boston Generative AI Records

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Massachusetts Public Records Request - Boston Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

I request records related to employee use of generative AI tools by the City of Boston, including any approved or actually used employee-facing systems described in the City's interim generative AI guidelines and related implementation materials.

We are requesting the following records:
1. All interaction logs, prompts, inputs, outputs, conversation histories, and similar records created by employees using any employee-facing generative AI chatbot tools approved, procured, or used by City of Boston employees, including ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, and any internally approved equivalents for the period from April 7, 2025 through the date this request is processed. Please provide these records in their native electronic format where available, including HTML exports, JSON, CSV, ZIP exports, or equivalent structured formats.
2. Any administrative usage logs, dashboards, reports, or other records sufficient to show which employees, offices, or units had access to these tools and the volume of usage during the same period.
3. Any policies, guidelines, training materials, approval documents, acceptable-use rules, or governance materials governing employee use of these tools or any other generative AI chatbot tools.
4. Any records retention, deletion, preservation, or disposition policies applicable to these interaction logs or related AI-generated records.

To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data, which generates a downloadable ZIP file containing conversations in HTML format. For Claude, export is similar, except via “Settings > Privacy > Export Data”. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console, the Purview compliance center, or individual user account settings. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/ (reporting on AI-generated government documents obtained through public records requests in Everett, WA); see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files (collecting records of AI usage at the Texas Department of Transportation released in response to a public records request).

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

If you contend that any portion of the requested records is exempt from disclosure, please identify the specific legal basis for the withholding and release all reasonably segregable non-exempt material. If any part of this request is unclear, I am happy to discuss ways to clarify or narrow it while preserving the core records sought.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jacqueline Xiong

communications.subject: City Public Records Request :: R002090-041526
communications.communication:
Dear , Thank you for your interest in public records of City of Boston. Your request has been received and is being processed. Your request was received in this office on 4/15/2026 2:22:02 PM and given the reference number R002090-041526 for tracking purposes. Department of Innovation and Technology 
PRL Office 
Room 703 
1 City Hall Square 
Boston, MA 02201-2021 

April 8, 2026 

To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records: 

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work. 

I request records related to employee use of generative AI tools by the City of Boston, including any approved or actually used employee-facing systems described in the City's interim generative AI guidelines and related implementation materials. 

We are requesting the following records: 
1. All interaction logs, prompts, inputs, outputs, conversation histories, and similar records created by employees using any employee-facing generative AI chatbot tools approved, procured, or used by City of Boston employees, including ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, and any internally approved equivalents for the period from April 7, 2025 through the date this request is processed. Please provide these records in their native electronic format where available, including HTML exports, JSON, CSV, ZIP exports, or equivalent structured formats. 
2. Any administrative usage logs, dashboards, reports, or other records sufficient to show which employees, offices, or units had access to these tools and the volume of usage during the same period. 
3. Any policies, guidelines, training materials, approval documents, acceptable-use rules, or governance materials governing employee use of these tools or any other generative AI chatbot tools. 
4. Any records retention, deletion, preservation, or disposition policies applicable to these interaction logs or related AI-generated records. 

To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data, which generates a downloadable ZIP file containing conversations in HTML format. For Claude, export is similar, except via “Settings > Privacy > Export Data”. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console, the Purview compliance center, or individual user account settings. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful. 

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses). 

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/ (reporting on AI-generated government documents obtained through public records requests in Everett, WA); see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files (collecting records of AI usage at the Texas Department of Transportation released in response to a public records request). 

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20. 

If you contend that any portion of the requested records is exempt from disclosure, please identify the specific legal basis for the withholding and release all reasonably segregable non-exempt material. If any part of this request is unclear, I am happy to discuss ways to clarify or narrow it while preserving the core records sought. 

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage. 

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Jacqueline Xiong 

View request history, upload responsive documents, and report problems here: 
https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2260908/ 

Filed via MuckRock.com 
E-mail (Preferred): 208417-93157371@requests.muckrock.com 

PLEASE NOTE OUR NEW ADDRESS 
For mailed responses, please address (see note): 
MuckRock News 
DEPT MR 208417 
263 Huntington Ave 
Boston, MA 02115 

PLEASE NOTE: This request is not filed by a MuckRock staff member, but is being sent through MuckRock by the above in order to better track, share, and manage public records requests. Also note that improperly addressed (i.e., with the requester's name rather than "MuckRock News" and the department number) requests might be returned as undeliverable. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. Again, thank you for using the Public Records Center. Director of Public Records 
Records Access Officer 
City of Boston 

Track the issue status and respond at: https://bostonma.govqa.us/WEBAPP//_rs/RequestEdit.aspx?rid=52747 (https://u8387778.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.VEsWrlMiJDg0mXafq1mEXTIDeIcW7QPGcxbg2r6mBl7V3dsh01U-2BpFnb5XYIdlqv4TBj33bkQUuSaK7U-2F6378Y8LYz-2FZAG-2FOkGflrY2-2BlQ8-3DzJY3_cDHuOYBty3S-2FQEiA56Gxwb3ERREARhhvewoExpnLrGesrBRL5-2FFSFKOmAPlo1ojsFm4rqpj5Dp8Sz6HjcLZfWox1g2ze2h6XFDgmWbH3WJNB4n27JLIUYh3SnnXOp9ONo7BTQh2ZLo-2BUzTKqGvKFw48VHMWcNUZ-2BesOn6N-2B-2FFYc2WFvDVL5yn8RO-2FvdxOOGnjdr1n2VOXeeKcazXFwNMP19Rb4OvYefYYnXUvo3oAlyGb2WcsBeh5soUQDYYWNIUytqDYXyB4oZ2IalBGVsgOSUNwy339r5B-2BE7tsGz8bmBDN0yiwOcBj1Avn-2FqkUZwm6pAfTv6C00P-2F2KOpVmk5l6JY625pfFEQhV-2F28LSxPn-2FdAR-2ByrbIxB-2FODkFDyCKNP)

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: City of Roanoke Rescue of Black-and-White Tuxedo Cat (Valiente)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

February 21, 2026

 FOIA Officer for the City of Roanoke, Virginia
robin.viohl@roanokeva.gov

Re: FOIA Request – October 27, 2024 Rescue of Black-and-White Tuxedo Cat (Valiente)

Dear FOIA Officer:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I request records related to the rescue, handling, and custody of a black-and-white tuxedo cat named Valiente at Towers Shopping Center, Roanoke, Virginia, on October 27, 2024. This request includes records in the custody of both the Roanoke Police Department (including Animal Control) and Roanoke Fire-EMS.
Please provide all records, electronic or paper, in the following categories:

1. Personnel Information
Names, badge numbers, and assignments of any Police officers present at the scene.
Any Animal Control officers notified, dispatched, or involved.
Names and assignments of Fire-EMS personnel, including Battalion Chief Robert Perdue and the responding firehouses.

2. Incident and Dispatch Records
CAD reports, dispatch logs, event summaries, and call detail records related to the incident.
Incident reports, after-action reports, or any internal reports generated by Police or Fire-EMS in connection with this event.

3. Body-Worn Camera / Audio / Video
All Police body-worn camera footage, dash camera footage, and radio communications/audio logs related to the rescue.
Any Fire-EMS audio, video, or operational recordings capturing the rescue or related communications.

4. Animal Handling / Custody Records
Intake logs, custody forms, or reports for Valiente on or about October 27, 2024.
Records showing which personnel or units had custody or transported the cat after the rescue.
Any documentation showing transfer to RCACP, veterinary providers, or other entities, including timing and personnel involved.
Records reflecting release, adoption, euthanasia, or continued custody of the cat.

5. Communications
Emails, memoranda, or written communications referencing Valiente.
Communications between Police, Fire-EMS, RCACP, or veterinary providers regarding the rescue, custody, or evaluation of the cat.

6. Metadata / Additional Notes
If records exist electronically, please provide metadata showing creation, modification, or access, to the extent easily available.
Please first confirm which records exist and provide an estimate of any fees before producing records, so that I may consider narrowing the request if necessary.
If separate fees apply for Police or Fire-EMS records, please notify me prior to processing.
If portions of records are exempt from disclosure, please identify the statutory exemption and provide all reasonably segregable portions.

Please note that during the incident, I may have used a different phone number than my current one; records associated with that number are responsive to this request.

Thank you for your assistance in fulfilling this request.

Respectfully, 

/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr.

Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., pro se
1328 Peachtree Street NE
Atlanta, Georgia 30309-3209
(404) 389-5642
csdelozier@pm.me

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr..

communications.subject: RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: City of Roanoke Rescue of Black-and-White Tuxedo Cat (Valiente)
communications.communication:
Subject: FOIA Request Follow-Up – Submitted February 21, 2026

Dear FOIA Officer,

I am following up on my Freedom of Information Act request submitted on February 21, 2026, via the MuckRock portal, regarding the rescue of Black-and-White Tuxedo Cat (Valiente).

Under Virginia Code § 2.2‑3704(B), public bodies are required to respond to FOIA requests within five working days, or within an extended timeframe if a written extension is properly issued. As of today, no official acknowledgment or response has been received through the MuckRock portal or other formal FOIA channels.

Please be advised:

Any correspondence sent outside the official FOIA submission channel (including private email) does not constitute a statutory acknowledgment or response.

This request is now past the statutory response timeframe. Failure to provide a proper written response may be treated as a deemed denial under Virginia law, which could give rise to formal legal action, including a petition in circuit court to compel compliance.

I respectfully request that your office provide a written acknowledgment and any required records or fee estimate immediately. Timely compliance will ensure that this request proceeds in accordance with Virginia law and is properly documented for use in related legal matters.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr., pro se

In the meantime, if you have any questions, please let me know.

 

Jessica Harper

City of Roanoke"

communications.subject: RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: City of Roanoke Rescue of Black-and-White Tuxedo Cat (Valiente)
communications.communication:
FOIA Request – Payment and Fire-EMS Records

Dear Ms. Harper,

Thank you for providing the cost estimate to fulfill my FOIA request regarding the rescue of Valiente on October 27, 2024. I would like to proceed with the request and cover the estimated fees. Could you please advise the preferred method of payment? For example, is there a portal for electronic payment, or should I submit a check or money order?

I also want to clarify the inclusion of Fire-EMS records. As noted in my request, multiple Fire-EMS units responded to the incident, including three fire trucks. Could you confirm which records exist for the responding personnel, units, and operational activities, and whether any additional fees apply for producing these records? My intent is to obtain all relevant documentation regarding the rescue and handling of Valiente on that date.

Thank you for your assistance, and I look forward to your guidance on completing this request.

Sincerely,

/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr.

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: City of Roanoke Rescue of Black-and-White Tuxedo Cat (Valiente)
communications.communication:
On March 2, 2026, you made a request for records in accordance with the Virginia Freedom of Information Act (VFOIA) wherein you requested various records related to a black-and-white tuxedo cat, Valiente,  and in incident at Towers Shopping Center, Roanoke, Virginia, on October 27, 2024. On March 6th, I advised of the costs associated with this request. You responded after close of business on March 9th, agreeing to the costs and advising the City to proceed with your request.

The responsive records from Roanoke City Fire-EMS (Fire-EMS), the Roanoke City Police Department (RPD), and E911 have been identified and are provided at this time. However, I am still working with the Animal Warden/Animal Control to determine if that Department has any records responsive to your request. Accordingly, it is practically impossible to provide the requested records, or determine whether they are available, within the five working days required by FOIA due to staffing and the time needed to compile and review responsive records. Therefore, the City invokes § 2.2-3704(B)(4) providing an additional seven working days to respond to your request. The deadline for the City to respond to your request is March 19, 2026.

Please see the City’s responses, in bold italics, in response to your requests below.

1. Personnel Information
Names, badge numbers, and assignments of any Police officers present at the scene.
Any Animal Control officers notified, dispatched, or involved.
Names and assignments of Fire-EMS personnel, including Battalion Chief Robert Perdue and the responding firehouses.

City’s Response to No. 1.
The names and assignments (position/rank) of the responding Fire-EMS and RPD personnel are contained in the NFIRS Report and Incident Details Report attached. Please be advised that the employee specific ID numbers have been redacted as that information is exempt from mandatory release pursuant to Virginia Code § 2.2-3705.1(1).
*Animal Warden/Animal Control records TBD.


2. Incident and Dispatch Records
CAD reports, dispatch logs, event summaries, and call detail records related to the incident.
Incident reports, after-action reports, or any internal reports generated by Police or Fire-EMS in connection with this event.

City’s Response to No. 2.
Please see the NFIRS Report and Incident Details Report attached in response to No. 1.
*Animal Warden/Animal Control records TBD.


3. Body-Worn Camera / Audio / Video
All Police body-worn camera footage, dash camera footage, and radio communications/audio logs related to the rescue.
Any Fire-EMS audio, video, or operational recordings capturing the rescue or related communications.

City’s Response to No. 3.
Please see the body-worn camera footage provided to you directly by the Roanoke Police Department. No additional video or audio from Fire-EMS, RPD, or E911 exist in response to your request.
*Animal Warden/Animal Control records TBD.


4. Animal Handling / Custody Records
Intake logs, custody forms, or reports for Valiente on or about October 27, 2024.
Records showing which personnel or units had custody or transported the cat after the rescue.
Any documentation showing transfer to RCACP, veterinary providers, or other entities, including timing and personnel involved.
Records reflecting release, adoption, euthanasia, or continued custody of the cat.

City’s Response to No. 4.
*Animal Warden/Animal Control records TBD.


5. Communications
Emails, memoranda, or written communications referencing Valiente.
Communications between Police, Fire-EMS, RCACP, or veterinary providers regarding the rescue, custody, or evaluation of the cat.

City’s Response to No. 5.
No responsive records from Fire-EMS, RPD, or E911 exist in response to your request.
*Animal Warden/Animal Control records TBD.



6. Metadata / Additional Notes
If records exist electronically, please provide metadata showing creation, modification, or access, to the extent easily available.

City’s Response to No. 6.
Please see responsive data, easily available, shown on the NFIRS and Incident Details Report attached in response to No. 1.
*Animal Warden/Animal Control records TBD.


Jessica Harper
Executive Administrative Assistant
FOIA Officer
Office of the City Attorney
City of Roanoke

communications.communication:
Hello Jessica Harper, 

Thank you for the update. I appreciate you continuing to work on the request and understand the need for the additional time. 

I also want to thank you for the records already provided from the Roanoke Police Department and for making the body camera footage available through the Axon digital platform. I look forward to receiving any additional responsive records as they become available.

Sincerely,

/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr.

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request #24-106616
communications.communication:
Mr. DeLozier -

I have spoken with the Animal Warden who has determined that no records exist responsive to your request as the Animal Warden did not respond to the October 27, 2024 call, as the Animal Wardens were off duty at the time of the call. Further, the Animal Warden has no record of responding the next day.

Additionally, Fire-EMS has provided the attached Incident History Report (“Dispatch Log”) showing a detailed history of the response to the October 27, 2024 incident.

For your convenience, I am attaching the NFIRS and Incident Details Report previously provided to you so that you have all of the City’s responsive records in one email.

If you have any questions, please let me know.

Have a great day!


Jessica Harper
Executive Administrative Assistant
FOIA Officer
Office of the City Attorney
City of Roanoke

communications.subject: RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request #RFD-24-00027934
communications.communication:
Subject: Follow-Up FOIA Request – Incident RFD-24-00027934 (October 27, 2024)

Dear FOIA Officer,

This is a follow-up request under the Virginia Freedom of Information Act regarding Incident RFD-24-00027934 (October 27, 2024), including all Fire-EMS and Police records related to the response and recovery efforts.

1. Certification of Completeness – Video Records
Please confirm whether all body-worn camera recordings, including any associated metadata, have been fully produced for all responding personnel during the incident timeframe of approximately 21:19 through 23:37 on October 27, 2024, including but not limited to:
	•	Battalion Chief units (BC1)
	•	Engine units (EN8ALS)
	•	Technical Rescue units (HTR6)
	•	Ladder/Aerial units (LA1)
	•	Any assisting or overlapping law enforcement personnel
If any additional recordings exist that have not been produced, please provide them.

If any portion has been withheld or redacted, please:
	•	identify the specific Virginia Code exemption relied upon; and
	•	state generally the nature of the withheld material.

2. Incident Video Footage (All Sources)
Please produce all responsive video recordings, regardless of device type, including:
	•	body-worn camera footage
	•	apparatus-mounted video
	•	helmet camera or personal recording devices used in official capacity
	•	any video later transferred to or retained by the agency
that depict or capture:
	•	the storm drain opening or interior view
	•	any visual observation of the animal involved
	•	any attempted extraction or rescue activity
	•	any handling, transfer, or post-extraction scene activity
	•	conditions immediately before or after termination of rescue operations
If no such footage exists, please confirm that no responsive video depicting these events was recorded or retained.

3. Photographs and Still Images
Please produce any photographs or still images taken during the incident by any responding personnel or agency devices, including those:
	•	captured on department-issued equipment
	•	transmitted to or stored within agency systems
	•	incorporated into incident records or reports

4. Audio Recordings (Radio + Dispatch)
Please produce all original audio recordings (not transcripts or logs) for:
	•	radio traffic
	•	dispatch communications
	•	tactical channel communications
for all responding units during the incident timeframe.

5. Animal Control / Custody Coordination
Please produce all records reflecting communication, attempted communication, or coordination with Animal Control or Animal Warden personnel, including:
	•	CAD notes
	•	radio communications
	•	phone logs or recordings
	•	emails, texts, or internal messaging systems
for October 27–28, 2024.

6. Follow-Up Activity (October 28, 2024 or Later)
Please produce all records reflecting:
	•	any return to scene activity
	•	follow-up rescue attempts
	•	coordination between Fire-EMS, Police, or Animal Control
	•	planned or attempted recovery operations
If no such records exist, please confirm no follow-up actions were documented.

7. Chain of Custody / Property Handling
Please produce all records reflecting the handling, retention, transfer, or disposition of any personal property provided or used during the incident, including but not limited to a drawstring laundry bag used during rescue operations.

8. Incident Participants (Including Identified Witnesses)
Please produce any records reflecting the involvement of all civilian or non-agency participants identified in incident records or body-worn camera footage, including individuals present at the scene during the response.

If any portion of this request is denied, please identify the specific statutory exemption and provide all reasonably segregable portions.
Thank you for your assistance.
/s/ Charles Stanley DeLozier Jr.

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request #RFD-24-00027934
communications.communication:
Mr. DeLozier –

In response to your follow-up request, I touched base with the Roanoke Police Department, Fire-EMS, the Animal Warden, and E911 to confirm that those Departments had captured all records responsive to your request. Fire-EMS was able to locate an email not previously provided that I have included here in response to #3 - an email from Battalion Chief Perdue to Chief Shannon regarding the rescue attempt on October 27, 2024. While records mention the intent to follow-up on October 28, 2024, the City has no records showing that Animal Control, the Roanoke Police Department, or Fire-EMS responded to follow-up on this situation on October 28, 2024, nor at any other subsequent time. For your convenience, I have attached all records previously provided to you on March 17, 2026. If you need the body camera resent, let me know and I will have the Roanoke Police Department send that to you.

The records attached to this email are:

  *   Email – October 28, 2024 from Battalion Chief Perdue to Chief Shannon
  *   Roanoke Police Department, Incident Details Report
  *   Fire-EMS NFIRS-1 Basic
  *   Fire-EMS NFIRS-1 Supplemental
  *   Fire-EMS Incident History Report

Portions of the records provided to you are exempt from mandatory disclosure, specifically employee ID numbers as that is personnel information, pursuant Virginia Code Section 2.2-3705.1(1), and have been redacted.

1. Certification of Completeness – Video Records
Please confirm whether all body-worn camera recordings, including any associated metadata, have been fully produced for all responding personnel during the incident timeframe of approximately 21:19 through 23:37 on October 27, 2024, including but not limited to:
• Battalion Chief units (BC1)
• Engine units (EN8ALS)
• Technical Rescue units (HTR6)
• Ladder/Aerial units (LA1)
• Any assisting or overlapping law enforcement personnel
If any additional recordings exist that have not been produced, please provide them.
If any portion has been withheld or redacted, please:
• identify the specific Virginia Code exemption relied upon; and
• state generally the nature of the withheld material.
Response: The Roanoke City Police Department provided you with the body worn camera of Office Plogger on March 10, 2026. No other body worn camera exists pertaining to this incident.

2. Incident Video Footage (All Sources)
Please produce all responsive video recordings, regardless of device type, including:
• body-worn camera footage
• apparatus-mounted video
• helmet camera or personal recording devices used in official capacity
• any video later transferred to or retained by the agency
that depict or capture:
• the storm drain opening or interior view
• any visual observation of the animal involved
• any attempted extraction or rescue activity
• any handling, transfer, or post-extraction scene activity
• conditions immediately before or after termination of rescue operations
If no such footage exists, please confirm that no responsive video depicting these events was recorded or retained.
Response: The Roanoke City Police Department provided you with the body worn camera of Office Plogger on March 10, 2026. No other body worn camera exists pertaining to this incident.

3. Photographs and Still Images
Please produce any photographs or still images taken during the incident by any responding personnel or agency devices, including those:
• captured on department-issued equipment
• transmitted to or stored within agency systems
• incorporated into incident records or reports
Response: Please see the October 28, 2024 email attached which includes two picture images of the incident area.

4. Audio Recordings (Radio + Dispatch)
Please produce all original audio recordings (not transcripts or logs) for:
• radio traffic
• dispatch communications
• tactical channel communications
for all responding units during the incident timeframe.
Response: The City has no audio records for this incident. Dispatch/E911 audio is stored for approximately six months, as this incident occurred approximately 19 months ago, the audio recordings no longer exist.

5. Animal Control / Custody Coordination
Please produce all records reflecting communication, attempted communication, or coordination with Animal Control or Animal Warden personnel, including:
• CAD notes
• radio communications
• phone logs or recordings
• emails, texts, or internal messaging systems
for October 27–28, 2024.
Response: Please see the records previously provided to you on March 17, 2026. For your convenience, I have attached the records to this email.

6. Follow-Up Activity (October 28, 2024 or Later)
Please produce all records reflecting:
• any return to scene activity
• follow-up rescue attempts
• coordination between Fire-EMS, Police, or Animal Control
• planned or attempted recovery operations
If no such records exist, please confirm no follow-up actions were documented.
Response: The City has no records documenting follow-up actions.

7. Chain of Custody / Property Handling
Please produce all records reflecting the handling, retention, transfer, or disposition of any personal property provided or used during the incident, including but not limited to a drawstring laundry bag used during rescue operations.
Response: Other than the records previously shared with you, the City has no separate chain of custody or property handling records for this incident.

8. Incident Participants (Including Identified Witnesses)
Please produce any records reflecting the involvement of all civilian or non-agency participants identified in incident records or body-worn camera footage, including individuals present at the scene during the response.
Response: Please see the records previously provided to you on March 17, 2026 as the names of City personnel who responded to the October 27, 2024 incident are contained within the records. For your convenience, I have attached the records to this email.

Jessica Harper
Executive Administrative Assistant
FOIA Officer
Office of the City Attorney
City of Roanoke

title: Faith Whittlesey

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Faith Whittlesey
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records relating to or mentioning  Faith Amy Ryan Whittlesey (February 21, 1939 – May 21, 2018), who served twice for a total of nearly 5 years as U.S. Ambassador to Switzerland and also served for 2 years on the Reagan White House Senior Staff as Assistant to the President for Public Liaison. 

The final House report on Iran-Contra concluded that Whittlesey unsuccessfully attempted to help Oliver North obtain a U.S. passport for a fake Saudi prince who claimed to have knowledge of the locations of hostages being held in Lebanon. In 1983, she established the White House Outreach Working Group on Central America to help increase private sector understanding of Reagan's policies, including working with, among many other individuals and groups, the American Security Council Foundation, to produce anti-Sandinista propaganda (what she would call "truth-telling") films, and the Council for National Policy to produce materials that revealed the Marxist–Leninist orientation of the Sandanista movement. Among the groups that participated in the Outreach Group effort was the AFL-CIO because "the Sandinistas were against free labor unions."

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.


Sincerely,

Emma Best

title: Logan County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Logan County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Maxine Brock
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
March 12, 2026

Logan County Clerk's Office
300 Stratton St # 311
Logan, WV 25601

To Whom It May Concern:

Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to Logan County opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting:
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 through today.
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 through today.
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from Logan County from March 1, 2025 until today.
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds

If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes.

The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records.

If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.
Thank you for considering my request.

Sincerely, Maxine Brock
maxieb112706@hotmail.com

Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to Logan County opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting:
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 through today.
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 through today.
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from Logan County from March 1, 2025 until today.
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds

If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public's understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records.

title: Frederick Wiseman (January 1, 1930 – February 16, 2026)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Frederick Wiseman (January 1, 1930 – February 16, 2026)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records mentioning, relating to, or generated as a result of investigation or inquiry into the filmmaker Frederick Wiseman (January 1, 1930 – February 16, 2026). His death is widely-known and widely reported; as such, privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. For example, you may find his New York Times obituary at the following link: https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/16/movies/frederick-wiseman-dead.html

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records of both HQ and all field offices for all relevant names, agencies, organizations, companies and events including but not limited to those cited in the previous paragraphs and/or links as well as a cross-reference with the Southern Poverty Law Center to include any information provided by the SPLC. My request includes but is not limited to 137, 157, 176, 177, 183, 184, 188, and 214 files. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

If any part of these documents are exempt from disclosure, please be sure to review and release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions of responsive documents, as the FOIA requires.

I request that I be properly classified as a media requester. I am an associate editor at Reason Magazine who has written numerous articles on topics of public interest for the Daily Beast, the Asia Times, Responsible Statecraft, the National Interest, America Magazine and the Armenian Weekly, and I am requesting these documents in an editorial capacity. Please contact me if you have any questions about my media status.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

database, please check back at a later date.

title: Wyoming DOC Prison-Month Level Data

communications.subject: Wyoming Public Records Act Request: Wyoming DOC Prison-Month Level Data
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wyoming Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

This is a request under the NA. I would like to request both that a document containing facility-month level data from January 2024 through December 2025 be provided on the below measures for every facility managed or owned by Wyoming Department of Corrections:

* Overall staff budgeted positions (per prison, per month)
* Overall staff filled positions (per prison, per month)
* Custody staff budgeted positions (per prison, per month)
* Custody staff filled positions (per prison, per month)
* Average hours of overall staff overtime (per prison, per month)
* Average hours of custody staff overtime (per prison, per month)
* Average hours of overall staff sick leave (per prison, per month)
* Average hours of custody staff sick leave (per prison, per month)
* Overall population count (per prison, per month)
* Total number of infractions (per prison, per month)
* Total number of violent infractions (including the department’s definition of which infractions are defined as violent) (per prison, per month)
* Total number in restricted housing (per prison, per month)
* Total number of appeals from incarcerated individuals (per prison, per month)

I am also requesting the name and email of the current, acting, and/or incoming wardens for each facility managed or owned by Wyoming Department of Corrections.
In order to help to determine my status to assess fees: I am affiliated with an educational institution, and this request is made for a scholarly or scientific purpose and not for commercial use.

Thank you for your consideration of this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jessie Harney

communications.subject: RE: Wyoming Public Records Act Request #26-209
communications.communication:
Hello! Thank you very much for sharing most of the data! 

I wanted to follow-up on the staffing specific variables in reference to the annual reports. It appears that this is reported aggregately over the year and not broken down by institution and month. Is it possible to get positions available/filled by month and institution?

Thanks!

title: 112 E. Burlington Ave, La Grange (Jackson Square)

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: 112 E. Burlington Ave, La Grange (Jackson Square)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

Dear FOIA Officer,

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140), I am requesting access to public records held by the Cook County Department of Environment and Sustainability regarding the development project at 112 E. Burlington Ave, La Grange, IL 60525 (the "Jackson Square" project).

Specifically, I am requesting the following records created or received between September 1, 2024, and March 16, 2026:

Demolition & Asbestos Permits: Any and all permit applications, approvals, or waivers for demolition or asbestos abatement. This includes, but is not limited to, the Demolition Debris Diversion (3D) Plan, asbestos inspection reports, and any "Letter of Approval" or "Letter of Waiver" issued for this site.

Correspondence: Any and all email correspondence, text messages, and internal memoranda regarding this property. Please search for communications involving department staff and the following external parties: the Village of La Grange, Five South Six, LLC, and any environmental consultants or demolition contractors associated with the project.

Inspection Records: Any reports or notes from site inspections, environmental complaints, or violation notices related to this address.

Fee Waiver  Status:
I am requesting a waiver of all fees as this request is not for a commercial purpose. I am seeking this information to monitor and report on environmental compliance and government transparency regarding a significant local development. 

Please provide these records in an electronic format to this email address. If any portion of this request is denied, please provide a written explanation citing the specific legal exemptions.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Concerned Citizen of La Grange

April 10, 2026 
RE: FOIA # R007519-040326 
Dear Ms. Citizen, 
The Office of the President of the Cook County Board of Commissioners is in receipt of your request for information under the Freedom of Information Act (the "Act"). 
On April 03, 2026, we received your request for the following: 
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

Dear FOIA Officer, 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140), I am requesting access to public records held by the Cook County Department of Environment and Sustainability regarding the development project at 112 E. Burlington Ave, La Grange, IL 60525 (the "Jackson Square" project). 

Specifically, I am requesting the following records created or received between September 1, 2024, and March 16, 2026: 

Demolition & Asbestos Permits: Any and all permit applications, approvals, or waivers for demolition or asbestos abatement. This includes, but is not limited to, the Demolition Debris Diversion (3D) Plan, asbestos inspection reports, and any "Letter of Approval" or "Letter of Waiver" issued for this site. 

Correspondence: Any and all email correspondence, text messages, and internal memoranda regarding this property. Please search for communications involving department staff and the following external parties: the Village of La Grange, Five South Six, LLC, and any environmental consultants or demolition contractors associated with the project. 

Inspection Records: Any reports or notes from site inspections, environmental complaints, or violation notices related to this address. 

Fee Waiver Status: 
I am requesting a waiver of all fees as this request is not for a commercial purpose. I am seeking this information to monitor and report on environmental compliance and government transparency regarding a significant local development. 

Please provide these records in an electronic format to this email address. If any portion of this request is denied, please provide a written explanation citing the specific legal exemptions. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Concerned Citizen of La Grange 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2257868/ 
Under the Act, a public body may extend the time to respond to a request by up to five (5) business days for a limited number of reasons.  5 ILCS 140/3(e).  We are extending the time to respond to your requests by five (5) business days because there is a need for consultation, which shall be conducted with all practicable speed, among two or more components of a public body having a substantial interest in the subject matter of the request.  5 ILCS 140/3(e)(vii). 

We will respond to your request by April 17, 2026. 
Thank you, 
Antonio

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request #R007519-040326
communications.communication:
Subject: RE: FOIA Request # R007519-040326 – Second Extension Not Authorized

Dear Ms. Dailey,

I am in receipt of your message from April 17, 2026, requesting a second extension of time to respond to my FOIA request.

Please be advised that under 5 ILCS 140/3(e), a public body is permitted to unilaterally extend the response time only once for a period not to exceed 5 business days. Your office has already utilized this statutory extension, which expired on April 17, 2026.

Under the Act, any further extension beyond the initial 10 business days requires a written agreement between the requester and the public body. I do not agree to an additional extension.

As the statutory deadline has passed without a production of records, Cook County is now in violation of the Act. Per 5 ILCS 140/3(d), please note that the public body:

May not impose any fees for the records provided; and

May not treat this request as "unduly burdensome."

I expect the responsive records to be produced immediately. If a full response is not received by the end of business on April 21, 2026, I will most likely proceed with a Request for Review through the Illinois Attorney General’s Public Access Counselor (PAC) and/or review the situation with an attorney to consider filing a lawsuit.

I am happy to work with the agency, but I am not understanding the difficulty behind the request and why more than 2 weeks of time is required to produce the results.    

Sincerely,
Concerned Citizen of La Grange
